title: Ibuki Maya in 30 and Beyond
wikitext:
[]: [Spoilers] Ibuki Maya in 3.0 and Beyond             Quote Post by bladerj » Thu Jan 17, 2013 7:51 pm Heres to everyone favorite geek tomboy and her magical netbook. So, does anyone thinks maya is still lesbian in this universe or just dont like imature men ? Does that mean hyuga will have a chance with her instead of crushing on Misato ? And what could had prompted her authoritarian atitude and change of heart ? What cruel fate awaits her ? You just know she is begging for a horrible death,i'm picturing her being the backstabed nerv personel from EoE :(

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Thu Jan 17, 2013 8:25 pm My guess? She tried hooking up with Ritsuko, but Ritsuko just wanted to be friends, and was always talking about Gendo. So now Maya has sworn off both guys and girls. Also 14 years living in a craphole world probably made her all bitter and stuff. But the first idea's funnier.

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Thu Jan 17, 2013 8:38 pm Actually there is a far greater transition to maya, if you go back to 1.0 and then 2.0 you see she is unable to do anything without ritsuko "consent" guidance,she is like Shinji unable to do stuff on her own. But in 3.0 she is commanding people, in a position of authority,something drastic must have happened, not just romance gone bad.

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Fri Jan 18, 2013 1:15 am When was it established in NGE that Maya was lesbian??

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jan 18, 2013 2:05 am K40s wrote: When was it established in NGE that Maya was lesbian??

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Fri Jan 18, 2013 4:49 am Now-Very-Good-At-Her-Job Maya? Toji Jr. Girl is the new her, and that pisses Maya off. It's not the young men that she really hates...

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Jan 18, 2013 5:30 am What, Maya's worried about Sakura stealing Ritsuko's heart? Talk about drama in the midst of chaos... I think that Maya either is lesbian and has feelings for Ritsuko which she hasn't yet revealed, or she just likes men the same age as her. Or older than her. Maybe Fuyutsuki still has a chance to get with Maya?

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Fri Jan 18, 2013 8:03 am She may be Bi, and Ritsuko is just the one she's most interested in.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Jan 18, 2013 9:40 am Didn't we hear just two lines from Maya in this film? I fear that any speculation about her sexual inclinations, relations, etc. is just as inconsistent as our speculations about what happened to the missing characters.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jan 18, 2013 2:04 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Didn't we hear just two lines from Maya in this film?.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Jan 18, 2013 2:19 pm I buy the fan-created/now-canon idea that Maya tried to take her relationship with Ritsuko to a whole new level & failed some time in the 14 year timeskip. I mean she was kicked off the bridge entirely.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jan 18, 2013 2:27 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: I mean she was kicked off the bridge entirely.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Fri Jan 18, 2013 4:10 pm Kendrix wrote: I would say that Midori, not Sakura took Maya's old spot as the naive token normal (though she's more clueless where Maya's reaction was more to be horrified), Sakura's a bit too "saintly" for "token normal.", she's the one who volunteered to look after the guy nobody else wanted to be in the same room with. Her reaction is treated as exceptional, not normal.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Jan 19, 2013 2:30 pm A.T. Fish wrote: Making her a badass also skyrocketed her sexiness to stratospheric levels.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Jan 19, 2013 7:45 pm She's absolutely bland in this film. There's barely any personality in her at all. We get perhaps three lines from her, but this amount is nowhere near enough to make any sort of emotional connection with her.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Jan 19, 2013 7:52 pm ^ It's basically a cameo appearance. Mostly there to tell the fans - who have already established some sort of emotional connection with her - how she has changed in the 14 years. Since the fans know who she is & how she acted in the past, seeing such a drastic change in her has a lot of personality. Hardly bland. Honestly, I read comments like that and I wonder if a lot of people on here even know how storytelling works. What are people expecting? Everything to come to a complete stop for a 15 minute monologue from Maya delivered straight to the audience about what she's been through the past decade & a half? Seeing the new Maya is a nice detail to enrich the world.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Jan 19, 2013 8:00 pm No, you misunderstand me. You don't need a monologue to feel connected to a character. I felt more of a connection to Midori than I did Maya, even though I knew Maya from the series, EoE, and the first two rebuild movies. This was because we were able to grasp aspects of Midori's nature through her speech and facial expressions. We got nothing from Maya except for a tad of anger. This says little about her character; all we can grasp is that bad things happened and made her mad. How childish of a description is that? With Midori, we can feel the childishness in her character. There's something for us to identify with.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sun Jan 20, 2013 5:10 am Kendrix wrote: I keep envisioning that scene where they first aquire the Wunder (which is sort of implied to have originally been a SEELE-creation) and the original bridge bunny trio storm a SEELE base kicking compious amounts of ass as the newbies like sakura, Midori and Hideki stare in awe.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 20, 2013 5:51 am Shinoyami65 wrote: As I recall in EoE she couldn't stand to fire a gun at another person...

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Sun Jan 20, 2013 6:53 am Shinoyame65 wrote: As I recall in EoE she couldn't stand to fire a gun at another person... I think her badassery kicked in after they got the Wunder, not before.
